---
title: "KalahZero"
subtitle: "Learn AlphaZero by building it for Kalah"
---

KalahZero is a teaching project for understanding AlphaZero from first
principles. The project pairs derivation-first tutorials with a compact Python
implementation of Kalah, baseline agents, Monte Carlo Tree Search, a PyTorch
policy-value network, self-play training, and evaluation tools.

The core loop is:

$$
\text{rules}
\rightarrow
\text{search}
\rightarrow
\text{training targets}
\rightarrow
\text{neural network}
\rightarrow
\text{stronger search}.
$$

## Start Here

Read the lessons in order:

::: {.callout-tip title="First lesson"}
Start with [Kalah Rules and State Representation](tutorials/01_kalah_rules.md).
The game engine is the foundation for every search and learning algorithm that
comes later.
:::

Play the browser version:

::: {.callout-note title="Graphical board"}
Open [the Kalah web app](web/index.qmd) to play against the browser-side
baseline agents.
:::

## What You Will Build

- An immutable Kalah game engine.
- Random, greedy, and minimax agents.
- UCT and AlphaZero-style PUCT search.
- A PyTorch policy-value network.
- A self-play training loop with replay buffer.
- Evaluation and inspection scripts.

## Local Commands

```bash
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pytest
python scripts/inspect_position.py --simulations 100 --tree-depth 2
python scripts/train.py --games 4 --simulations 25 --epochs 2
```

## Why This Site Exists

GitHub's Markdown renderer does not display all LaTeX math consistently in
plain `.md` files. Quarto renders the tutorials as a website with MathJax, so
equations such as

$$
\mathcal{L}(\theta)
=
(z - v_\theta(s))^2
- \sum_a \pi(a \mid s)\log p_\theta(a \mid s)
+ \lambda \lVert \theta \rVert_2^2
$$

display properly in the browser.
